summary: Follow-up to PR #2125 that matched and documented a large block of VS Records diagram code, especially mndiagram2, then applied the resulting matching lessons back to mndiagram. The PR replaced many address-based mnDiagram2 symbols with semantic names, added Diagram2-related types/prototypes/static declarations, filled out mndiagram2.c with matched functions, and adjusted mndiagram.c implementation details for better matching. The author states the mndiagram2 function and struct names were based on validation in Dolphin debugger.
what_changed: Expanded src/melee/mn/mndiagram2.c from a tiny stub-like file into a large matched implementation, with about 1391 added lines according to the PR slice.

Renamed mnDiagram2 address-based symbols in config/GALE01/symbols.txt to semantic functions such as mnDiagram2_IsTimeStat, mnDiagram2_IsDistanceStat, mnDiagram2_IsPercentageStat, mnDiagram2_IsIconOnlyStat, mnDiagram2_ClearStatRows, mnDiagram2_UpdateHeader, mnDiagram2_HandleInput, mnDiagram2_GetStatValue, mnDiagram2_CreateStatRow, mnDiagram2_PopulateStatRows, mnDiagram2_UpdateScrollArrows, mnDiagram2_Create, mnDiagram2_Init, mnDiagram2_GetRankedFighter, mnDiagram2_GetRankedName, mnDiagram2_GetAggregatedFighterRank, and mnDiagram2_ClearDetailView.

Added mndiagram2 static/header declarations and a Diagram2 forward declaration, indicating that the mndiagram2 code now had explicit local static data and typed user data instead of relying only on unknown addresses.

Added or updated Diagram2-related structs in src/melee/mn/types.h, with the diff excerpt showing fields such as row_labels, row_values, row_icons, header_text, mode headers, selection indices, scroll_offset, and name/fighter mode state in use.
Changed src/melee/gm/gm_1601.h prototype for gm_80160C90 from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to a concrete signature: void gm_80160C90(HSD_Text* text, u8 fighter_id, s32 arg2).

Reworked mndiagram.c formatting and ranking/helper logic to improve matching, including inline helper splits for name/fighter fall sums, decimal/time/string formatting loops, prev/next name and fighter index parameter typing, and ranked fighter tie/all-zero handling.
mndiagram.c started including mndiagram2.static.h and gm/gmmain_lib.h, implying reuse of mndiagram2 discoveries or shared stat/menu state details.
smart_moves: Used semantic names in the symbol map for every newly understood mndiagram2 function, turning a dense run of mnDiagram2_80243A3C-style labels into searchable behavior names.

Validated mndiagram2 functions and structs in Dolphin debugger before naming them, according to the PR body, which is a strong practice for menu/UI decomp work where field meaning often depends on runtime object layout.

Separated stat classification helpers into clear boolean functions: IsTimeStat, IsDistanceStat, IsPercentageStat, and IsIconOnlyStat. This makes later stat formatting and row rendering code easier to reason about and search.

Introduced small inline helpers and wrappers where they improved matching, such as mnDiagram2_RefreshStatRows, mnDiagram_GetEntityByIndex, mnDiagram_SumNameFalls, mnDiagram_SumFighterFalls, CheckAllZeroPlayTime, and CountTiedFighters.

Preserved known matching-sensitive patterns such as explicit casts to u8, use of signed s32 loop indices for prev/next navigation, PAD_STACK where needed, and local helper split-outs to control stack/codegen.
Applied lessons from matching mndiagram2 back to mndiagram instead of treating the two files independently, which helped improve older mndiagram matches.
decomp_lessons: For menu decomp, runtime validation in Dolphin debugger can justify naming UI object fields and function purposes, especially for GObj user data, JObj containers, HSD_Text objects, row arrays, header objects, and mode state.
When converting address-named symbols, update config/GALE01/symbols.txt in the same PR so future searches and cross-file references use the new semantic names.
Small static inline helper functions can be a matching tactic, not just a readability choice; they can isolate stack padding, repeated sums, or refresh logic while preserving generated code.
Signed-versus-unsigned parameter choices matter for matching navigation logic. The diff changes several prev/next index helpers from u8 to s32 parameters while still returning u8.
Use explicit u8 casts when indexing persistent name/fighter data if the original code truncated loop counters or entity IDs.
Tie-handling and all-zero sentinel cases in ranking functions should be modeled explicitly. This PR uses sentinel fighter/name result values such as 0x19 when no unique ranked fighter is available.
Concrete library prototypes discovered from callsites should be pushed into headers, as with gm_80160C90, so future matching can avoid UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS and improve type propagation.
naming_conventions: mnDiagram2_* functions were renamed from address-based labels to PascalCase verb phrases under the existing module prefix, e.g. mnDiagram2_ClearStatRows, mnDiagram2_UpdateHeader, mnDiagram2_HandleInput, mnDiagram2_GetStatValue.
Boolean stat classifier names use the Is*Stat pattern: mnDiagram2_IsTimeStat, mnDiagram2_IsDistanceStat, mnDiagram2_IsPercentageStat, mnDiagram2_IsIconOnlyStat.
Lifecycle and GObj/user-data functions use direct action names: InitUserData, FreeUserData, Create, Init, Think, OnAnimComplete.
Ranking helpers use GetRanked* and GetAggregated* naming for VS Records rank lookups: mnDiagram2_GetRankedFighter, mnDiagram2_GetRankedName, mnDiagram2_GetAggregatedFighterRank.
The PR keeps module prefixes distinct: mnDiagram for the first VS Records diagram page, mnDiagram2 for the second, and mnDiagram3 for the third.
Struct/type names observed in the diff include Diagram2, mnDiagram2_SortEntry, JObjContainer, Diagram2DetailView, RankEntry, AnimLoopSettings, CountEntry, Diagram, and CSSData.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK in functions where stack shape needed to match, including examples in mndiagram.c and mndiagram2.c.
Introduced static inline helpers to influence inlining and stack/register behavior while making code more understandable.
Kept some wrapper macros around index functions, such as mnDiagram_GetNameByIndex_s and mnDiagram_GetFighterByIndex_s, apparently to control signedness or cast behavior in generated code.
Used a union mnDiagram2_SortEntry with f64 and u64 views for 64-bit sorting operations, suggesting a matching tactic for sort/key manipulation.
Changed pointer-walking prev/next fighter index functions to operate with signed indices and pointer decrement/increment, matching original bounds behavior.
Rewrote number/time/string formatting loops to index directly into buffers instead of pointer increments, which the diff suggests improved matching.
Split fall-total calculations into static inline sum helpers plus public wrapper functions with PAD_STACK, likely to preserve call-visible stack layout while sharing cleaner loop bodies.
Reviewer feedback pointed out that switch cases should use enum values directly, reinforcing that semantic enum constants are preferred over raw numeric cases once stat-type enums are known.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.comment: Why not just use the enum values in the case?
review_feedback.lesson: Prefer enum constants in switch cases when the enum is known; this improves readability and validates semantic stat naming.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs touching src/melee/mn/mndiagram2.c to see whether the Dolphin-validated Diagram2 field names were refined.
Search for remaining fn_80243D40-style or mnDiagram3 address-based labels around the VS Records diagram modules.
Look for PRs that replace raw VSSTAT numeric cases with enum constants after ribbanya's review feedback.
Search gm_80160C90 callsites to confirm the concrete prototype propagated correctly across menu code.
Compare PR #2125 with PR #2129 to identify which mndiagram2 matching lessons were applied back to mndiagram.
